active_ingredients: Ciprofloxacin 250 mg, 500 mg, 750 mg tablets; 500 mg and 1000 mg extended-release tablets; 250/5 mL and 500/5 mL oral suspensions; IV solution; otic and ophthalmic forms (e.g., Cipro) - prescription only
indications_and_usage: Indicated for the treatment of various bacterial infections caused by susceptible organisms - complicated and uncomplicated urinary tract infections and pyelonephritis, complicated intra-abdominal infections (in combination with metronidazole), skin/skin-structure infections, bone and joint infections, infectious diarrhea, typhoid fever, inhalational anthrax (post-exposure), and plague. It is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic. Fluoroquinolones are reserved for situations in which other treatment options are not appropriate due to a profile of serious adverse effects (see boxed warning). Prescription only.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to ciprofloxacin or other fluoroquinolones; concurrent administration with tizanidine; pediatric patients except for select serious infections.
boxed_warning: Fluoroquinolones, including ciprofloxacin, have been associated with disabling and potentially irreversible serious adverse reactions that have occurred together, including tendinitis and tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, central nervous system effects, and exacerbation of myasthenia gravis. Discontinue at the first sign of any of these reactions. Because fluoroquinolones have been associated with serious adverse reactions, reserve ciprofloxacin for use in patients who have no alternative treatment options for acute bacterial sinusitis, acute exacerbation of chronic bronchitis, and uncomplicated urinary tract infections.
warnings_and_cautions: Tendinitis/tendon rupture (especially Achilles, higher risk in age >60, in transplant recipients, and with corticosteroids); peripheral neuropathy (may be permanent); CNS effects (seizures, increased intracranial pressure, psychosis, anxiety, confusion, insomnia, suicidal ideation, hallucinations); myasthenia gravis exacerbation (avoid); aortic aneurysm/dissection risk (especially in older adults and those with aortic aneurysm history); QT prolongation; hepatotoxicity (sometimes severe); photosensitivity; serious hypersensitivity including anaphylaxis; hypoglycemia and hyperglycemia (more in diabetics); Clostridioides difficile colitis; crystalluria (maintain hydration); developmental musculoskeletal effects in juveniles (caution in pediatric use). Adjust dose in renal impairment.
adverse_reactions: Nausea, diarrhea, vomiting, abdominal pain, rash, headache, restlessness, dizziness; rare but serious - tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, severe CNS effects, hepatotoxicity, aortic events, QT prolongation, anaphylaxis, photosensitivity, hypoglycemia.
drug_interactions: Tizanidine - contraindicated (CYP1A2 inhibition - severe hypotension/sedation); theophylline - markedly raised levels (toxicity); warfarin - raised INR; antacids, calcium, iron, zinc, sucralfate, dairy/fortified juice, and oral multivitamins reduce absorption (separate by 2 hours before or 6 hours after); QT-prolonging drugs; NSAIDs (CNS stimulation); sulfonylureas/insulin (hypoglycemia); cyclosporine (nephrotoxicity); methotrexate (raised levels); probenecid (raised levels); typhoid vaccine.
dosage_and_administration: Adults: uncomplicated UTI - 250 mg twice daily for 3 days; pyelonephritis/complicated UTI - 500 mg twice daily for 7-14 days (or 1000 mg ER once daily); intra-abdominal infection (with metronidazole) - 500 mg twice daily; respiratory/other infections - 500-750 mg twice daily for the prescribed duration. Renal impairment - adjust per CrCl. Take with or without food (avoid taking simultaneously with dairy products or calcium-fortified juice alone); maintain adequate hydration; do not crush ER tablets. Complete the full course.
overdosage: Overdose may cause renal injury (crystalluria), seizures, GI symptoms, QT prolongation; supportive care, hydration; hemodialysis removes only a small fraction. Seek emergency care; call Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
